setting.note: A single hot summer on the Côte d'Azur, between Cannes and Saint-Tropez, where a seventeen-year-old girl, her widowed father and his lovers idle at a rented white villa above a pine-fringed cove. The map runs along the real Mediterranean coast and the red cliffs of the Estérel, from the resort towns of the drives and the nights out to the corniche where the summer turns to tragedy.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Françoise Sagan, first published 1954. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Bonjour Tristesse, Françoise Sagan (1954). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep blue of the Mediterranean below the villa. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
